title: Dipak Jaiswal vs Securities And Exchange Board Of India ... on 7 November, 2024
body: Dipak Jaiswal vs Securities And Exchange Board Of India ... on 7 November, 2024 Author: Gurpal Singh Ahluwalia Bench: G. S. Ahluwalia NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-JBP:54995 JBP:54995 1 M.P. No.5880/2024 IN THE HIGH COURT OF MADHYA PRADESH AT JABALPUR BEFORE HON'BLE SHRI JUSTICE G. S. AHLUWALIA ON THE 7th OF NOVEMBER, 2024 MISC. PETITION No. 5880 of 2024 DIPAK JAISWAL Versus SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE BOARD OF INDIA (SEBI) SEBI BHAWAN AND OTHERS Appearance: Shri Puneet Shroti - Advocate for petitioner. ORDER This petition under Article 227 of Constitution of India has been filed against order dated 11.09.2024 passed by 28th District Judge, Jabalpur in Case No.284/2022 by which application filed by petitioner under Section 137 C.P.C. for supply of Hindi indi translation of written statement filed by defendant No.1, of application filed under Order 7 Rule 11 C.P.C . and annexures and for supply of Hindi translation of written statement filed fil by respondents Nos.5 to 7 has been rejected. 2. It is submitted that since defendant No.1 and defendants Nos.5 to 7 have filed their written statements in English and an applicati application under Order 7 Rule 11 C..P.C. has also been filed by defendant No.1 which is in English and since petitioner is Hindi indi knowing person, therefore, he filed an application under Section 137 1 C.P.C. for supply of translated NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-JBP:54995 JBP:54995 2 M.P. No.5880/2024 copy of aforesaid documents but by the impugned order, the said application has been rejected 3. Challenging the order passed by the Court below, it is submitted by counsel for petitioner that petitioner does not know English and for proper understanding of written statements statement as well as ave averments made in application filed under Order 7 Rule 11 C.P.C . C as well as contents of documents, it is essential essential for him to go through the H Hindi version of pleadings and therefore, Trial Court committed a material illegality by rejecting the application plication filed fil under Section 137 C.P.C. 4. Heard learned counsel for petitioner. 5. Section 137 of C.P.C. C reads as under: "Section Section 137 - Language of subordinate (1)The Courts.-(1) The language which, on the commencement of this Code, is the language of any Court subordinate to a High Court shall continue to be the language of such subordinate Court until the State Government otherwise directs. (2) The State Government may declare what shall be the language of any such Court and in what character applications to and proceedings proceedings in such Courts shall be written. (3) Where this Court requires or allows anything other than the recording of evidence to be done in writing in any such Court, such writing may be in English; but if any party or his pleader is unacquainted with English a translation anslation into the language of the Court shall, at his request, be supplied to him; and the Court shall make such order as it thinks fit in respect of the payment of the costs of such translation." translation. 6. From plain reading of aforesaid section, it is clear that if any party or his pleader is unacquainted with English, a translation into the language of the Court shall, shall at his request, be supplied to him. NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-JBP:54995 JBP:54995 3 M.P. No.5880/2024 7. It is not the case of petitioner that his counsel is not an English knowing person. However, it is submitted submitted by counsel for petitioner that petitioner has a suspicion whether his counsel would be in a position to apprise him about the contents of pleadings in the same sense or not, therefore, he wanted to go through and understand the pleadings on his own n so that he can file an effective reply to the application filed under Order 7 Rule 11 C.P P.C. 8. Counsel for petitioner was directed to point out the mark mark-sheets of petitioner to show that he had never gone to an English nglish medium school or he was never taught ght English subject during his schooling days. 9. Counsel for petitioner fairly conceded that no such document has been filed. 10. There is another important aspect which cannot be lost sight of. The present petition has been drafted and filed in English language. The affidavit has been signed by petitioner thereby giving a declaration that contents have been read over to him in Hindi and he has understood the same. The petitioner has signed the affidavit in English. Above all, the petition has been filed by the same counsel, who is appearing for petitioner in the Trial Court. It is really surprising that petitioner has relied ied upon the translation of the writ petition told to him by his counsel but he is not ready to rely on the translation of pleadings of defendants Nos.1 and 5 to 7.. Furthermore, Vakalatnama which has been filed alongwith the writ petition is also in English. English. Thus, it is clear that petitioner must have signed the Vakalatnama after understanding the contents of the same. 11. So far as disbelief expressed by petitioner against his counsel namely Jitendra Pandey is concerned, it appears that it is frivolous frivolous. In NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-JBP:54995 JBP:54995 4 M.P. No.5880/2024 fact petitioner is interested in somehow getting the matter delayed so that the application filed under Order 7 Rule 11 C.P. C .C. may not be decided. 12. There is another shocking aspect of the matter which indicates that petitioner is interested in somehow getting the matter delayed by adopting the delaying tactics. Petition has been argued by Shri Punit Shroti. The Vakalatnama which has been uploaded on the website of the High Court clearly indicates that it does not bear the signatures of petitioner. When Shri Shroti was confronted with this position position, then he submitted that he has been authorized by his colleague and therefore, his colleague Shri Satish Yadav, who was holding the Vakalatnama of petitioner has allowed him to appear alongwith him. This endor endorsement made by Shri Satish Yadav does not amount to engaging Shri Punit Shroti as a counsel for petitioner. 13. The Vakalatnama which has been filed by Shri Puneet Shroti, Advocate reads as under: VAKALATNAMA IN THE HIGH COURT OF MADHYA PRADESH PRINCIPAL SEAT AT JABALPUR MISCELLANEOUS LLANEOUS PETITION NO.5880 OF 202 2024 PETITIONER DIPAK JAISWAL VERSUS RESPONDENT SECURITIES & EXCHANGE BOARD OF INDIA (SEBI) & OTHERS I/We the Appellant/Petitioner/Plaintiff/Claimant/ Applicant or Respondent/Non-Applicant/Defendant Respondent/Non Applicant/Defendant nname below do hereby appoint, engage, and authorize advocate(s) named below to appear, act and plead in aforesaid case/proceedings, which shall include applications for restoration, setting aside of ex-parte ex parte orders, NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-JBP:54995 JBP:54995 5 M.P. No.5880/2024 corrections, modifications, review and recall recall of orders passed in these proceedings. In this court or in any other court in which the same may be tried/heard/proceeded with and also in the appellate revisional or executing court in respect of proceedings arising from this case/proceedings as per agreed terms and conditions and authorize him/them to sing and file pleadings, appeals cross objections, petitions, applications, affidavits, or other documents as may be deemed necessary or proper for the prosecution/defense of the said case in all its st stages and also agree to ratify and confirm acts done by him/them as if done by me/us. In witness whereof I/we do here unto set my/our hand to these present, the contents of which have been duly understood by me/us, this 15th day of November 2024 at Jabalpur. No.5880/2024 14. Vakalatnama which was signed by petitioner thereby appointing O.P. Mishra, Jitendra Pandey, Satish Yadav and Arnav Tiwari, Advocates reads as under: VAKALATNAMA IN THE HIGH COURT OF MADHYA PRADESH PRINCIPAL SEAT AT JABALPUR MISCELLANEOUS LLANEOUS PETITION NO.5880 OF 202 2024 PETITIONER DIPAK JAISWAL VERSUS RESPONDENT SECURITIES & EXCHANGE BOARD OF INDIA (SEBI (SEBI) & OTHERS I/We the Appellant/Petitioner/Plaintiff/Claimant/ Applicant or Respondent/Non-Applicant/Defendant Respondent/Non Applicant/Defendant name below do hereby appoint, engage, and authorize advocate(s) named below to appear, act and plead in aforesaid case/proceedings, which shall include applications for fo restoration, setting aside of ex-parte parte orders, corrections, modifications, review and recall of orders passed in these proceedings. In this court or in any other court in which the same may be tried/heard/proceeded with and also in the appellate revisional revisional or executing court in respect of proceedings pro arising from this case/pro roceedings as per agreed terms and and conditions and authorize him/ him/them to sing and file pleadings, appeals cross objections, petitions, ons, applications, affidavits, or or other documents as mayy be deemed necessary or proper for the prosecution/defense defense of the said case in all its stages and also agree to ratify rat and confirm acts done by him/them/them as if done by me/us. In witness whereof I/we do here unto set my my/our hand to these present, the contents contents of which have been duly understood by me/us, this 15th day of November 2024 at Jabalpur. Om Prakash Dixit Pride 9424685293 Mishra Jabalpur En. No. 1494/19 /1988 4. Jitendra Pandey Dixit Pride 9826436617 En. No.1360/2006 06 Jabalpur, 5. Satish Yadav Dixit Pride 9407221211 En.No.781/2007 Jabalpur 6. Arnav Tiwari Dixit Pride 7389999951 En.No.2362/2017 Jabalpur 15. This Vakalatnama does not give any jurisdiction to counsel to engage any other counsel without knowledge and authority by petitioner. Since Vakalatnama filed by Shri Punit Shroti does not bear any authority by petitioner, therefore, even otherwise, this Court cannot consider the submission made by Shri Shroti. 16. At this stage, Shri Arnav Tiwari, Advocate who holds Vakalatnama signed by petitioner appears. 17. Be that whatever it may be. NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-JBP:54995 JBP:54995 8 M.P. No.5880/2024 18. Since petitioner had miserably failed in prima facie establishing that he is not well conversant with English, therefore, it is clear that application under Section 137 C.P.C. C was filed with a solitary intention to delay the disposal of application filed under Order 7 Rule 11 C C.P.C . 19. Accordingly, this Court is of considered opinion that Trial Court did not commit any mistake by rejecting the application filed under Section 137 C.P.C. 20. Thus, the petition fails and is hereby dismissed. (G.S. S.
